text0: Two young girls sit traumatized in front of a destroyed house, the younger one having placed her head into the lap of her older sister. A young boy is leaning against a beam in complete apathy.
text1: As Mel approaches the scene of total war destruction, with his boys and Heath, he shudders to think what has happened.
text2: Every part of his beautiful plantation home is burned down and in total ruins. As Mel draws closer, he sees his young son dead in front of the house, killed by a gunshot wound to his chest.
text3: Mel kneels down to talke a close look at his killed boy
text4: his bloodied hand showing that he clutched unto death some of his favorite military tin soldier pieces, representing what his father went to war for.
text5: Mel is totally distraught and destroyed over the death of his young son and pledges revenge against whoever the perpetrator is.
text6: Heath understands the deep meaning of this scene of horror and destruction of a family's happiness, and ponders his place in the war against the English and life.
text7: As the family buries their young son, a deep sadness befalls the family as they all ask themselves what other cost there will be extracted from them as part of this upheaval.

text0: A juror is trying not to fidget during the trial.
text1: The defendant is trying not to fidget in her best uncomfortable hat.
text2: The judge formally reads out the charges.
text3: He says that he's going to remind the jury to make an unbiased decision.
text4: The defendant glances nervously behind her at the jury.
story: A juror is trying not to fidget during the trial. The defendant is trying not to fidget in her best uncomfortable hat. The judge formally reads out the charges. He says that he's going to remind the jury to make an unbiased decision. The defendant glances nervously behind her at the jury.
